features: Crater ulcers starting at the tips of the interdental papillae
Cervical lymphadenopathy and fever in advanced cases

investigations: Smears from ulcers show predominantly spirochaetes and gram-negative fusiform bacteria

drug: Metronidazole - Adult: 200 mg orally 8 hourly for 3 days
Metronidazole - Child: 1-3 years: 50 mg orally every 8 hours for 3 days
Metronidazole - Child: 3-7 years: 100 mg every 12 hours for 3 days
Metronidazole - Child: 7-10 years: half adult dose
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Metronidazole: nausea, vomiting, unpleasant taste; disulfiram-like reaction with alcohol
supportive_measures: Ascorbic acid - Adult: not less than 250 mg orally daily (in divided doses)
Ascorbic acid - Child: 1 month - 4 years: 125 - 250 mg in 1-2 divided doses
Ascorbic acid - Child: 4-12 years: 250-500 mg daily in 1-2 divided doses
Ascorbic acid - Child: 12-18 years 500 mg - 1 g daily in 1-2 divided doses
Ferrous sulphate - Adult: 200 mg orally three times daily taken before food
Ferrous sulphate - Child 6-12 years: half adult dose

differential_diagnoses: Gingival ulceration in acute leukaemia or aplastic anaemia
definitive_treatment: Once the acute phase has subsided, oral hygiene should be brought to as high a standard as possible to lessen the risk of recurrence
